---
title: "Edible Plants Dataset Analisis (TidyTuesday) - Data Cleaning and Preparation"
author: "Leonardo Cárdenas Peralta, Daniel Enrique Molina Sanglade and Enrique Fernandez Salmeron"
date: "April-May 2026"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    theme: cosmo
    highlight: tango
---

# Introduction

## Setup
```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Cargamos las librerías necesarias para el análisis.
# Nota: La gestión de versiones se realiza mediante renv.
library(tidyverse)
library(tidytuesdayR)
library(janitor)
library(skimr)
library(here)
#library(tidymodels)
library(performance)
library(kableExtra)
```

## Downloading the dataset

Now we have to downlaod the dataset to start working with it

```{r Option 1, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
tuesdata <- tidytuesdayR::tt_load('2026-02-03')

edible_plants <- tuesdata$edible_plants
```

```{r Option 2, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
edible_plants <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2026/2026-02-03/edible_plants.csv')
edible_plants
```

```{r Option 3, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Used to upload the file to Github

download.file(
  url = 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2026/2026-02-03/edible_plants.csv',
  destfile = "Data/edible_plants.csv"
)

# Used to have the dataset locally from Github

edible_plants <- read_csv(here("Data", "edible_plants.csv"))
```

## Checking the dataset
```{r, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
# Summary of all the columns of the dataset
glimpse(edible_plants)

# Numeric summary of the numeric columns of the database
skim(edible_plants)
```

# Data Cleaning and Preparation

In this section we clean the original dataset to correct inconsistencies and transform text variables into analyzable numerical data.

```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# 1. Name normalization and cleaning

# We are using janitor to parse the name of the categorical variables so they all have the same structure.
# Then we are using tidyverse to edit the content of the columns
edible_plants_clean <- edible_plants %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~str_trim(str_to_lower(.))))
```

```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# 2. Correcting inconsistencies and typo-errors
# This is going to help in the misspelling of "perrenial" and "perennial"
edible_plants_clean <- edible_plants_clean %>%
  mutate(
    cultivation = recode(cultivation, "brassicas" = "brassica"),
    season = case_when(
      str_detect(season, "perrenial|perannial") ~ "perennial",
      str_detect(season, "biennial") ~ "biennial",
      TRUE ~ season
    ),
    temperature_class = recode(temperature_class, "very hard" = "very hardy"),
    sunlight = str_replace_all(sunlight, "full sun/partial shade/ full shade", "full sun/partial shade/full shade")
  )
```

```{r}
write_csv(edible_plants_clean, here("Data", "edible_plants_cleaned.csv"))
```
